user_message: Give me the deployment blockers for my team. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 13:24 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: VanArsdel Ltd deployment blockers

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-13 15:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: deployment blockers Review

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:59 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is deployment blockers.

[00:07:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:09:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:24:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:26:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:30:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-G trap (Misattributes attendee role); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-G -- Misattributes attendee role
